authoritative_sources.title: 42 CFR Part 484 - Home Health Services
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: The eCFR is the primary public source for Medicare HHA Conditions of Participation and payment-related quality requirements that constrain field-quality, patient-rights, care-planning, coordination, clinical-record, and QAPI evidence review.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: home-health regulatory baseline and release-boundary triage
used_for: HHA Conditions of Participation and quality assessment baseline
care-plan, coordination, clinical-record, and patient-rights boundary handling

authoritative_sources.title: Home Health Agencies
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS is the federal certification and compliance authority for HHAs; this page is authoritative for HHA survey, certification, and interpretive-guideline context.
used_for: authority boundary between quality support and formal certification decisions

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Benefit Policy Manual, Chapter 7 - Home Health Services
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS's Medicare Benefit Policy Manual is authoritative for home-health eligibility, certification, face-to-face, plan-of-care, skilled-need, and homebound prerequisites that gate administrative quality handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: eligibility, referral, certification, and plan-of-care prerequisite review
used_for: coverage, referral, certification, and authorization dependency framing
clinical-versus-administrative boundary around skilled need and homebound status

authoritative_sources.title: Medicare Claims Processing Manual, Chapter 10 - Home Health Agency Billing
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS's claims manual is authoritative for home-health billing, NOA, claim, consolidated billing, remittance, denial, and claim-processing dependencies that may affect quality disposition and payer handoff.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: claim, remittance, denial, and payer-handoff evidence review
used_for: remittance, denial, and appeal artifact awareness
boundary between quality findings and revenue-cycle or payer-operations authority

authoritative_sources.title: Home Health Quality Reporting Requirements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS defines HH QRP submission obligations, OASIS and HHCAHPS reporting requirements, quality-data compliance consequences, and public-review posture for HHAs.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: quality reporting, OASIS, HHCAHPS, and compliance evidence review
used_for: HH QRP evidence and submission expectations
quality-reporting hold, release, and escalation scenarios

authoritative_sources.title: Home Health Quality Measures
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS defines home-health measure categories and quality data sources, including OASIS, claims, and HHCAHPS sources used in quality reporting and public comparison.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: measure evidence and field-quality findings review
used_for: OASIS, claims, and patient-experience source mapping
evidence completeness checks for findings summaries

authoritative_sources.title: Internet Quality Improvement and Evaluation System (iQIES) for Home Health Agencies is Now Available
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS QTSO identifies iQIES as the HHA OASIS assessment submission and reporting system, making it authoritative for assessment-system evidence dependencies.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: OASIS submission and system-of-record dependency review
used_for: iQIES as an OASIS submission and reporting system

authoritative_sources.title: Home Health Care CAHPS (HHCAHPS)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS identifies HHCAHPS as the standardized, publicly reported patient-experience survey for Medicare-certified home health agencies and links it to quality improvement and incentive programs.

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Necessary Requirement
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, Office for Civil Rights
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR administers HIPAA Privacy Rule guidance; this source constrains PHI access, disclosure, and request handling for quality support work.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: PHI-sensitive communication and privacy escalation review

authoritative_sources.title: The Home Health Value-Based Purchasing (HHVBP) Model
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS defines the HHVBP quality incentive model and performance-report context for Medicare-certified HHAs.
used_for: commercial and operating relevance for field-quality evidence

workflow_stages.name: service support intake and scope triage
workflow_stages.description: Identify request type, patient/member or episode scope, agency branch, payer, plan, jurisdiction, service dates, referral, authorization, consent, requested disposition, and whether the request stays inside field-quality service support.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: patient/member and claim truth-set assembly
workflow_stages.description: Collect the authoritative eligibility, benefits, referral, authorization, plan-of-care, order, encounter, visit, OASIS, iQIES, claim, remittance, denial, complaint, and corrective-action records needed before a field-quality conclusion.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: field-quality evidence verification
workflow_stages.description: Check whether records support the requested service disposition, including start-of-care or resumption-of-care timing, face-to-face and order evidence, missed-visit or complaint facts, OASIS submission status, HH QRP measure context, HHCAHPS feedback, and claim-status implications.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
Public payer or provider operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: resolution, communication, and hold or release handling
workflow_stages.description: Produce a bounded resolution note, communication summary, quality finding, hold, release recommendation, or unresolved-risk note only when evidence and authority are sufficient; otherwise request missing prerequisites or escalate.
source_classes: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: exception, PHI, clinical, payer, and corrective-action escalation
workflow_stages.description: Route policy exceptions, PHI-sensitive requests, patient-safety signals, clinical judgment, payer interpretation, claim approval, coding, utilization management, repeat defects, and unsupported closure attempts to the correct adjacent specialist or human reviewer.
source_classes: Public payer or provider operating guidance

artifacts: eligibility, benefits, referral, prior authorization, payer portal, and patient/member access records
home-health referral packet, SOC or ROC record, plan of care, orders, face-to-face encounter note, visit notes, clinical-administrative notes, and discharge or transfer records

OASIS assessment status, iQIES validation or submission reports, HH QRP measure workpapers, HHCAHPS summaries, HHVBP performance reports, complaint logs, missed-visit logs, incident records, and corrective-action evidence
charge capture, coding, claim, NOA, remittance, denial, appeal, and payer correspondence records
tenant authority maps for jurisdiction, service model, payer, plan, clinical reviewer, privacy owner, payer-policy owner, final release approver, and systems-of-record locations
systems_of_record: EHR or EMR and associated clinical documentation systems
home-health agency management and scheduling systems
OASIS and iQIES assessment submission and reporting systems
practice management, revenue cycle, billing, remittance, denial, or payer portal systems
case management, utilization review, CRM, patient/member access, complaint, incident, and document-control systems

allowed: classify the request as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked on missing home-health prerequisites
request or validate minimum tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context required to continue safely
prepare evidence-backed service dispositions, communication summaries, quality findings, next-step packages, and corrective-action escalation notes without overstating authority
determine whether a support request can be resolved directly under documented service rules, needs clarification, or must be escalated
apply documented quality, service, or policy rules without improvising exception authority
escalate: missing eligibility, benefits, referral, authorization, service-date, consent, payer, plan, patient/member, episode, branch, system, or authority facts would change the outcome
PHI-sensitive request requires privacy-controlled handling or minimum-necessary review
patient-safety, clinical-risk, licensed review, payer-policy, medical-necessity, coding, utilization-management, revenue-cycle, legal, compliance, or approval authority is required
CMS, state Medicaid, payer, accreditation, tenant policy, or source freshness conflicts materially change release, closure, quality reporting, privacy, claim, or corrective-action posture
repeat field failures or unresolved corrective actions require human review
refuse: override policy, pricing, eligibility, referral, authorization, coverage, or payer limits to resolve a case
imply diagnosis, treatment planning, homebound determination, skilled-need judgment, medical necessity, or licensed clinical judgment
change payer-policy interpretation, privacy obligations, coding, utilization-management, claim payment, or financial approval
release or close quality work without required evidence, review support, approval authority, or corrective-action verification
disclose PHI or clinical details without established authorization and minimum-necessary basis

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: bad routing or unsupported closure creates patient/member harm
domain_failure_modes.trigger: field quality evidence is incomplete but the lane releases work or misses clinical-risk escalation
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: preserve hold posture, cite missing evidence, and route to licensed clinical, patient-safety, or quality owner

domain_failure_modes.trigger: visit notes, OASIS, diagnosis, complaint, or claim information is shared without consent, role authorization, or minimum-necessary basis
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: limit communication, document privacy boundary, and escalate to healthcare privacy or compliance owner

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: revenue leakage, claim denial, or compliance exposure
domain_failure_modes.trigger: eligibility, authorization, order, face-to-face, OASIS, NOA, claim, coding, or payer rule dependency is ignored
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: block resolution until authoritative records are retrieved and route payer or revenue questions to the correct specialist

domain_failure_modes.failure_mode: generic quality handling ignores home-health artifacts
domain_failure_modes.trigger: the lane relies on generic ticket metrics without OASIS, plan-of-care, visit, authorization, complaint, claim, or HHQRP context
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: require home-health-specific source records and document the public baseline

domain_failure_modes.trigger: quality support output decides homebound status, skilled need, treatment plan, medical necessity, payer appeal outcome, or coverage interpretation
domain_failure_modes.mitigation: refuse the decision, preserve administrative evidence, and route to licensed clinical review, utilization management, revenue cycle, or payer operations

required_workflow_questions.question: What prerequisites or authorizations gate the workflow?
required_workflow_questions.answer: Patient/member identity, agency branch, service dates, referral, eligibility, benefits, prior authorization where applicable, payer and plan rules, consent or permitted-use basis, orders, plan of care, face-to-face documentation, OASIS/iQIES status, and tenant approval maps gate field-quality resolution.

required_workflow_questions.question: Which records or systems establish the patient, member, or claim truth set?
required_workflow_questions.answer: EHR/EMR, home-health agency management and scheduling systems, OASIS/iQIES, practice management, revenue cycle, payer portals, case management, utilization review, CRM, complaint, incident, and document-control systems establish the truth set.

required_workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and licensed clinical, privacy, or payer-interpretation authority begin?
required_workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops before diagnosis, treatment planning, homebound or skilled-need judgment, medical necessity, payer-policy interpretation, claim approval, coding, utilization-management decisions, PHI disclosure authorization, privacy obligation interpretation, legal reportability, and final release or policy exceptions.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
Customer or stakeholder requests a policy exception
Service request lacks the detail needed for safe resolution
Communication becomes a handoff to another lane
Evidence is incomplete for release or signoff
A user tries to close quality work without review support

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: state Medicaid, licensure, and accreditor overlays
unresolved_ambiguities.description: State Medicaid programs, state HHA licensure rules, accreditation standards, and payer contracts may add requirements beyond the public CMS/HHS baseline.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Do not guess local rules; request jurisdiction, payer, accreditation, and tenant policy context when they materially affect the result.

unresolved_ambiguities.description: Public sources describe prerequisites, but patient-specific homebound, skilled need, treatment planning, and medical necessity require licensed or payer-authorized review.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Refuse clinical or payer determination and route to licensed clinical review, utilization management, or payer operations.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: tenant system names and retrieval hooks
unresolved_ambiguities.description: System names, record locations, and approval owners differ by tenant and cannot be inferred from public sources.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant systems-of-record map and retrieval hooks before case-specific resolution.
